questions.question: Which of the following statements best describes the Indian Union Cabinet’s role in legislation?
questions.option_1: The Cabinet gives final shape to government bills and prepares the legislative agenda.
questions.option_2: The Cabinet enacts laws without any involvement of any house of the Indian Parliament.
questions.option_3: The Cabinet is the only law-making body in India.
questions.option_4: The Cabinet has judicial authority over legislations surpassing the judiciary.

questions.question: How many cities were initially included under the Smart Cities Mission launched by the Government of India?
Following the provisions of the 74ᵗʰ Constitutional Amendment, 1992, which of the following terms is used for urban governance in areas transitioning from rural to urban?
Which institution has been established by the 74ᵗʰ Constitutional Amendment, 1992, to conduct municipal elections in each State?
When were the Municipal Corporations of Calcutta and Bombay established?
What major power did the 74ᵗʰ Amendment Act, 1992, provide to Urban Local Bodies?
Which of the following changes did the Waqf (Amendment) Act, 2025 make?

Which of the following is INCORRECT based on the Supreme Court’s ruling in 'Samtola Devi vs The State Of Uttar Pradesh', on 27 March 2025, regarding the ‘Maintenance and Welfare of Parents and Senior Citizens Act, 2007’?
questions.option_1: Eviction of a child or relative is mandatory in every case of neglect under the Act.
questions.option_2: Senior citizens can file for maintenance even after transferring their property to their legal heirs.
questions.option_3: The Act does not provide any provision for the eviction of a child or relative.
questions.option_4: Tribunals under the Act have the authority to order the eviction of a child or relative if it is deemed necessary for the protection of the senior citizen.

questions.question: How does the writ jurisdiction of the High Courts differ from that of the Supreme Court?
questions.option_1: High Courts require prior approval from the Supreme Court before issuing writs.
questions.option_2: High Courts can issue writs only in appellate cases, not in original jurisdiction.
questions.option_3: High Courts can issue writs only against state governments, not the Centre.
questions.option_4: High Courts can issue writs for purposes beyond Fundamental Rights, unlike the Supreme Court.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the Supreme Court’s writ jurisdiction is correct?
questions.option_1: It can only be invoked for enforcement of Fundamental Rights.
questions.option_2: It allows the Court to issue writs only against the Central Government.
questions.option_3: It extends to both Fundamental Rights and other legal rights.
questions.option_4: It is exclusive and cannot be exercised by the High Courts.

questions.question: Which type of subordinate court primarily handles disputes related to property ownership and contractual agreements?
The Supreme Court can issue writs under Article 32 only for which specific purpose?
questions.option_3: To review decisions of statutory tribunals
questions.option_4: To enforce the Directive Principles of State Policy

questions.question: Which urban localself governmentbody manages small towns with limited municipal functions like street lighting and drainage?
Which of the following Urban Local Government Bodies in India was discontinued after 1992?
Which among the following significant changes was introduced in the Waqf (Amendment) Act, 2025?
questions.option_1: Exemption of Waqf properties from legal scrutiny
questions.option_3: Mandating representation of at least two Muslim women on Waqf Boards
questions.option_4: Abolition of the Central Waqf Council

questions.question: In the ministerial hierarchy in India, Ministers of State generally have:
questions.option_1: no executive responsibility in any of the portfolios
questions.option_3: secondary rank within the portfolios assigned to Cabinet Ministers
questions.option_4: equal standing with Cabinet Ministers in their assigned portfolios

questions.question: The Supreme Court described itself as the "sentinel on the qui vive" in relation to ____________.
Which of the following is a key reason for judicial review in India?
questions.option_1: To ensure the supremacy of the Parliament
questions.option_2: To protect the supremacy of the Constitution and fundamental rights
questions.option_3: To allow the judiciary to make laws
questions.option_4: To maintain federal balance between Centre and States

questions.question: In India, what are municipalities primarily responsible for?
questions.option_2: Administration of small cities or towns

questions.question: Which is NOT an advantage of a Lok Adalat?

Which of the following statements regarding the Supreme Court's interim order in the Ranveer Allahbadia case (February 2025) is/are correct? The Supreme Court granted interim protection to Allahbadia but harshly criticised his remarks as obscene and vulgar. The Court ruled that Allahbadia was free to travel abroad without any restrictions since the remarks were made behind a paywall.
Which of the following statements is true about the Permanent Lok Adalat under Section 22-B of The Legal Services Authorities Act, 1987?
questions.option_1: The award of the Permanent Lok Adalat is not binding on the parties.
questions.option_2: The Permanent Lok Adalat has jurisdiction over disputes related to criminal offences.
questions.option_3: The Permanent Lok Adalat can handle cases up to a certain monetary limit (currently ₹1 crore).
questions.option_4: The Permanent Lok Adalat can only act as a conciliator and cannot decide disputes.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about Small Causes Courts is correct?
questions.option_1: They deal exclusively with criminal cases of a minor nature.
questions.option_2: Their decisions are final but subject to High Court’s revision.
questions.option_3: They can hear appeals from Nyaya Panchayats.
questions.option_4: They exist in all states under a uniform nomenclature.

questions.question: Who among the following will be the 25ᵗʰ Chief Justice of India in May 2025?
Which of the following is NOT a category of judicial review, as classified by Justice Syed Shah Mohammed Quadri?
questions.option_1: Judicial review of judicial appointments to the Supreme Court and High Courts
questions.option_2: Judicial review of administrative action of the Union and State and authorities under the state
questions.option_3: Judicial review of legislation of the Parliament and State Legislatures and subordinate legislations
